reasoning: The US military strategy in Yemen currently relies entirely on air strikes and arming/supporting local proxy forces, as evidenced by operations in March-May 2025. Ground operations are being planned by Saudi/UAE-backed Yemeni forces, and political reporting confirms that US ground troops are considered "highly unlikely." The resolution criteria explicitly exclude special operations forces, military advisors, and contractors—those categories represent the vast majority of any small-footprint US presence that might foreseeably be required. For regular, non-SOF military personnel to enter the terrestrial territory of Yemen, a major policy shift (which contradicts the current administration's stance) or an unexpected crisis (such as a downed pilot requiring a Marine TRAP team recovery) would be needed. With no indication of an imminent policy change and the highly restricted scope of the question, the probability remains very low, resting alongside the Polymarket estimate of ~0.09 but adjusted slightly lower due to mounting clarity that the upcoming proxy ground offensive will not involve regular US infantry.
